objective: Close the native-size Remap-MMAPI size boundary by testing whether a 640x360 Regular05 input can be padded to a WarpGrid-compatible 640x368 pitch-linear scratch stage, processed by VPI Remap, cropped/transformed back to the native 640x360 encoder main chain, and remain readable without green output, tearing, or inflated claims.

entry_context.project_positioning: Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization, with EIS as the representative workload.
entry_context.previous_operator_result: VPI C++ Remap works on CPU/CUDA, and NV12_ER CPU/CUDA works in the standalone probe.
entry_context.previous_mmapi_result: Remap can replace PerspectiveWarp in a minimal MMAPI/VPI/NVENC diagnostic sample when the input is padded to 640x368. Native 640x360 failed because VPI WarpGrid aligned height to 368 and Remap requires output image size to match the warp map.
entry_context.previous_quality_bridge_result: Static single-cell local Remap correction did not improve the selected parallax/global-warp boundary and is not a current quality route.
entry_context.accepted_device_path: H264 input -> MMAPI/NVDEC block-linear NV12 main chain -> NvBufSurfTransform to pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch -> VPI CUDA stage -> NvBufSurfTransform back to block-linear NV12 -> NVENC.
entry_context.current_question: Can the native 640x360 source keep the encoder-facing main chain at 640x360 while only the VPI Remap scratch stage is padded to 640x368 and cropped back after Remap?
entry_context.quality_boundary: This is a size/layout/dataflow diagnostic. It does not change resid_r15_s07, does not reopen EIS tuning, and does not claim mesh/local-warp quality.

frozen_variables: Regular05 native 640x360 source remains the primary input
MMAPI decode and NVENC encoder-facing main chain remain native 640x360 block-linear NV12
VPI scratch stage is pitch-linear NV12_ER
scratch padding target is WarpGrid-compatible 640x368 unless the patch logs a different VPI requirement

result.decision: The native-size Remap-MMAPI size boundary is closed for a minimal diagnostic path: keep the encoder-facing 640x360 main chain native, pad only the pitch-linear VPI scratch stage to 640x368, run Remap, then transform/crop back to 640x360 before NVENC.
result.claim_boundary: Diagnostic operator/dataflow evidence only. No Regular EIS quality improvement, no mesh/local-warp quality, no zero-copy, and no full-pipeline acceleration.

success_criteria: no green output or visible block tearing is observed in generated review asset
identity mode does not add large edge black-border beyond the source/dataflow baseline
timing logs include input transform, wrapper/remap/sync, output transform, total stage, and running average
documentation separates native-size pad/crop dataflow evidence from Regular EIS quality and full-pipeline acceleration
